data.title: Reassessing Spotify (SPOT) After Its Run And Podcast And Audiobook Expansion
data.description:
• If you are wondering whether Spotify Technology's share price still offers value after its recent run, this article will help you make sense of what the current market is actually pricing in.
  • The stock last closed at US$511.70, with a 0.1% move over the past week, a 12.4% decline over the past month, an 11.0% decline year to date, and a 3.6% decline over the last year, while the 3 year and 5 year returns stand at 354.0% and 48.3% respectively.
  • Recent headlines around Spotify have focused on its push into podcasts and audiobooks, licensing deals with major labels, and ongoing debates about creator payouts. These developments help frame how investors think about its potential and risks, and they sit in the background as the market reassesses what is a reasonable price to pay for the business today.
  • On Simply Wall St's valuation checks, Spotify Technology scores 4 out of 6, which means it screens as undervalued on four metrics and less so on two. Next, we will look at how different valuation methods line up on that score before finishing with a way to put those numbers in a broader context.

Find out why Spotify Technology's -3.6% return over the last year is lagging behind its peers.

Advertisement

Approach 1: Spotify Technology Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) Analysis

A Discounted Cash Flow, or DCF, model takes Spotify Technology's projected future cash flows and discounts them back to today, aiming to estimate what the entire business may be worth right now.

In this model, Simply Wall St uses a 2 Stage Free Cash Flow to Equity approach, starting from Spotify's last twelve months free cash flow of about €2.9b. Analyst inputs cover the next few years. Beyond that period, Simply Wall St extends the projections, with free cash flow expected to reach €7.6b in 2030 based on those extrapolations.

All those future cash flows, expressed in euros, are discounted back to today and summed to arrive at an estimated intrinsic value of €849.54 per share. Compared with the current share price of US$511.70, the model suggests the stock trades at a 39.8% discount to this estimate. On this DCF view, this points to Spotify being materially undervalued according to the model's assumptions.

Result: UNDERVALUED (based on this DCF model)

Our Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) analysis suggests Spotify Technology is undervalued by 39.8%. Track this in your watchlist or portfolio, or discover 876 more undervalued stocks based on cash flows.

Head to the Valuation section of our Company Report for more details on how we arrive at this Fair Value for Spotify Technology.

Approach 2: Spotify Technology Price vs Earnings

For a profitable company like Spotify, the P/E ratio is a useful shorthand for how much investors are currently paying for each unit of earnings. It ties the share price directly to the bottom line, which is often what ultimately matters most for equity holders.

What counts as a “normal” P/E depends on how the market sees growth potential and risk. Higher expected earnings growth or lower perceived risk can support a higher P/E, while slower growth or higher uncertainty tends to align with a lower P/E. Spotify currently trades on a P/E of 62.25x, compared with the Entertainment industry average of 24.18x and a peer group average of 74.24x. This means the stock is priced above the wider industry but below peers on this metric.

Simply Wall St’s Fair Ratio of 34.84x is a proprietary estimate of what Spotify’s P/E might be given its earnings growth profile, industry, profit margins, market cap and risk factors. This tailored yardstick can be more informative than a simple comparison to peers or industry averages because it adjusts for Spotify’s specific characteristics rather than treating all companies as equivalent. With the current P/E of 62.25x sitting well above the Fair Ratio of 34.84x, this multiple based view points to the shares looking overvalued relative to that framework.

Result: OVERVALUED
